experiments.reasoning: The passage describes a systematic observation of a luminous phenomenon in a preserved animal (likely a veal or calf) over multiple days. The author notes the progression of light visibility, mentions the apparatus (curtains, observation of light decay and reappearance), and conducts repeated observations under controlled conditions (e.g., morning/night, after sunrise). This fits the criteria of an experiment due to the deliberate, repeated, and methodical recording of results over time.
experiments.description: The experiment involved observing the luminous properties of a preserved animal (possibly a veal or calf) over seven days. The author recorded the light's visibility during different times of day (morning, night, after sunrise) and noted its decay and reappearance. The observations were conducted in a controlled environment (likely indoors, given the mention of curtains and indoor lighting decay).
experiments.quote: ‘the light had begun to decay and the offensive smell to grow somewhat strong: which seems to argue, that the disposition, upon whose account our Veal was luminous, may very well consist both with its being and not being in a state of putrefaction’ (emphasis added for clarity).
experiments.outcome: The light was observed to fade and reappear over time, with the author noting that it shone better on the fifth day and was barely visible on the seventh day. The observations suggest a temporal decay of luminosity, though the exact cause (putrefaction vs. another mechanism) was not definitively resolved.
experiments.location: Indoors (likely within the author's residence, given the mention of curtains and observation of light decay in a confined space).
